fields.name: Ian Saunders
fields.title: Secretary General
fields.bio:
Ian Saunders is Secretary General of the World Customs Organization (WCO) in January 2024. With more than three decades in international relations, he is known for advancing global customs modernization and promoting inclusive, rules-based trade cooperation.

Key facts
Position: Secretary General, World Customs Organization (since 2024)

Nationality: United States

Education: Georgetown University; executive programs at Harvard Kennedy School and American University

Prior roles: Deputy Assistant Secretary, U.S. Department of Commerce; senior executive, U.S. Customs and Border Protection

Experience: 30+ years in customs, trade, and international policy

Career and background
Before his election, Saunders served as Deputy Assistant Secretary for the Western Hemisphere at the U.S. Department of CommerceÔÇÖs International Trade Administration. There, he led programs and strategies to strengthen U.S. commercial partnerships across the Americas. Earlier, at U.S. Customs and Border Protection, he held senior posts including Assistant Commissioner for International Affairs and Acting Deputy Executive Assistant Commissioner for Operations Support. He joined the U.S. Senior Executive Service in 2008. 

Leadership at the World Customs Organization
Elected in June 2023 and assuming office in January 2024, Saunders heads the WCOÔÇÖs efforts to secure and facilitate global trade, protect revenue, and modernize customs operations worldwide. His priorities include balancing tradition with innovation, promoting transparency and inclusivity, and strengthening global supply-chain resilience amid climate, health, and digital challenges. 

Vision and impact
Saunders emphasizes cooperative border management, technological innovation, and diverse participation across the WCOÔÇÖs 185 member administrations. Drawing on his long-standing work on frameworks such as the SAFE Framework of Standards, he advocates pragmatic governance that aligns customs modernization with security and sustainable development goals.

fields.title: WCO Global Canine Forum
fields.title_fr: Forum mondial canin de lÔÇÖOrganisation mondiale des douanes (OMD)
fields.theme: Agility in a Complex World: Advancing Canine Services through Innovation, Security, and Collaboration.
fields.theme_en: Agility in a Complex World: Advancing Canine Services through Innovation, Security, and Collaboration.
fields.theme_fr: Agilit├ę dans un monde complexe : faire progresser les services cynophiles gr├óce ├á lÔÇÖinnovation, ├á la s├ęcurit├ę et ├á la coop├ęration.
fields.date_location_fr: Du 7 au 9 septembre 2026 ÔÇô Tachkent, Ouzb├ękistan

fields.heading: Overview
fields.body:
Join the international Canine (K-9) and Customs community in Tashkent, Uzbekistan, from 7ÔÇô9 September 2026 for a global forum dedicated to the development of service dog training and modern border security practices.

The Global Canine Forum (K-9) will bring together Customs administrations, border and law enforcement agencies, experts and partners to exchange experience, demonstrate operational capabilities and discuss the future of canine services in border management. The forum is organized with the participation of the World Customs Organization (WCO) and the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan and will serve as a platform for professional dialogue, practical demonstrations and international cooperation in the field of service dog training.
fields.body_en:
Join the international Canine (K-9) and Customs community in Tashkent, Uzbekistan, from 7ÔÇô9 September 2026 for a global forum dedicated to the development of service dog training and modern border security practices.

The Global Canine Forum (K-9) will bring together Customs administrations, border and law enforcement agencies, experts and partners to exchange experience, demonstrate operational capabilities and discuss the future of canine services in border management. The forum is organized with the participation of the World Customs Organization (WCO) and the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan and will serve as a platform for professional dialogue, practical demonstrations and international cooperation in the field of service dog training.
fields.body_fr:
Rejoignez la communaut├ę internationale des unit├ęs cynophiles (K-9) et des douanes ├á Tachkent, en Ouzb├ękistan, du 7 au 9 septembre 2026, ├á lÔÇÖoccasion dÔÇÖun forum mondial consacr├ę au d├ęveloppement de la formation des chiens de service et aux pratiques modernes de s├ęcurit├ę aux fronti├Ęres.

Le Forum mondial des unit├ęs cynophiles (K-9) r├ęunira des administrations douani├Ęres, des services des fronti├Ęres et des forces de lÔÇÖordre, ainsi que des experts et partenaires, afin dÔÇÖ├ęchanger des exp├ęriences, de pr├ęsenter des capacit├ęs op├ęrationnelles et de discuter de lÔÇÖavenir des services cynophiles dans la gestion des fronti├Ęres. Organis├ę avec la participation de lÔÇÖOrganisation mondiale des douanes (OMD) et du Comit├ę des douanes de la R├ępublique dÔÇÖOuzb├ękistan, le forum constituera une plateforme de dialogue professionnel, de d├ęmonstrations pratiques et de coop├ęration internationale dans le domaine de la formation des chiens de service.

fields.body: The forum will also include practical demonstrations and exhibition areas where participants will be able to observe the operational capabilities of service dogs, explore training equipment and learn about modern approaches used in canine centers around the world. These demonstrations will provide valuable insights into how service dog teams support border protection, law enforcement operations and rescue missions.

fields.name: Central Office
fields.description: An authorized law enforcement body in the field of customs administration under the Ministry of Finance of the Republic of Uzbekistan. Its main functions include ensuring compliance with customs legislation, maintaining statistics on foreign trade and cross-border movement of goods, and combating smuggling.

Ses principales fonctions consistent ├á assurer le respect de la l├ęgislation douani├Ęre, ├á tenir des statistiques sur le commerce ext├ęrieur et le mouvement transfrontalier des marchandises, ainsi quÔÇÖ├á lutter contre la contrebande.

fields.name: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.name_en: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.name_fr: The National Canine Center of the State Customs Committee
fields.description:
The canine service of the State Customs Committee was established in October 1993. The National Canine Center, founded on October 24, 1996, by a resolution of the Cabinet of Ministers, serves as the main institution for training canine specialists and service dogs.**

The Center provides methodological support to customs and other law enforcement agencies, conducts initial and advanced training for canine handlers, and is responsible for breeding, selecting, and training service dogs.

Located in the Sergeli district of Tashkent, the Center features modern facilities, including training grounds, a kennel, a veterinary unit, and educational departments equipped for both theoretical and practical instruction.

Service dogs are trained in key areas such as narcotics detection, explosives and weapons detection, counter-terrorism, tracking, protection, and search-and-rescue operations. Work is also underway to train dogs in detecting foreign currency.

The Center maintains an automated information system for monitoring canine operations and uses electronic microchipping to manage detailed data on each service dog.

Its specialists regularly exchange experience with international partners, and the Center also provides training for foreign canine service representatives. To date, more than 30 training manuals have been developed.

In 2008, the Center was granted the status of a Regional Canine Center by the World Customs Organization, confirming its compliance with international standards. Its activities have been highly evaluated by international experts.

Service dogs trained at the Center have played a significant role in detecting illegal trafficking of narcotics, psychotropic substances, weapons, and ammunition.
fields.description_en:

fields.name: Customs Institute
Customs border post
fields.description:
A customs post is a unit of the customs authority empowered to carry out full-scale customs clearance and customs control at a specific location or within a designated area. It does not have the status of a legal entity.

A customs post is usually located away from the main customs administration and operates within the jurisdiction of a subordinate customs authority. Its activities typically cover one or two districts of a region, as well as transport infrastructure facilities such as small river ports, airports, and railway stations. Customs posts are established at nearly every border crossing along the state frontier. They may also be set up within large industrial enterprises.

A customs post is primarily responsible for the inspection and clearance of goods.

The structure of a customs post includes the head of the post and divisions organized on a territorial basis. The divisionsÔÇÖ staff typically consists of a division head, a senior customs service inspector, senior inspectors, and inspectors.

fields.title: Center for Islamic Civilization
fields.description:
The Center for Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan under the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan (Arabic: ┘ůě▒┘âě▓ ěž┘äěşěÂěžě▒ěę ěž┘äěąě│┘äěž┘ů┘Őěę ┘ü┘Ő ěú┘łě▓ěĘ┘âě│ě¬ěž┘ć ┘äě»┘ë ┘ůěČ┘äě│ ěž┘ä┘łě▓ě▒ěžěí ┘äěČ┘ů┘ç┘łě▒┘Őěę ěú┘łě▓ěĘ┘âě│ě¬ěž┘ć; English: Center of Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan under the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan) is a research institution in Uzbekistan engaged in scientific studies related to Islamic history.

On March 17, 2026, under the leadership of Shavkat Mirziyoyev, on the eve of Eid al-Fitr, the official opening ceremony of the Center was held. On April 13, 2026, it was included in the Guinness World Records in the category of ÔÇťthe largest Islamic civilization museum.ÔÇŁ

History

The Center for Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan was established in accordance with the Presidential Decree of the Republic of Uzbekistan dated July 23, 2017, issued by Shavkat Mirziyoyev. Over more than eight years, this large complex was constructed and fully completed. On March 17, 2026, on the eve of Eid al-Fitr, the Center was officially inaugurated.

fields.description_fr:
Le Centre de civilisation islamique en Ouzb├ękistan, relevant du Cabinet des ministres de la R├ępublique dÔÇÖOuzb├ękistan (en arabe : ┘ůě▒┘âě▓ ěž┘äěşěÂěžě▒ěę ěž┘äěąě│┘äěž┘ů┘Őěę ┘ü┘Ő ěú┘łě▓ěĘ┘âě│ě¬ěž┘ć ┘äě»┘ë ┘ůěČ┘äě│ ěž┘ä┘łě▓ě▒ěžěí ┘äěČ┘ů┘ç┘łě▒┘Őěę ěú┘łě▓ěĘ┘âě│ě¬ěž┘ć ; en anglais : Center of Islamic Civilization in Uzbekistan under the Cabinet of Ministers of the Republic of Uzbekistan), est une institution de recherche en Ouzb├ękistan engag├ęe dans des ├ętudes scientifiques relatives ├á lÔÇÖhistoire islamique.

Le 17 mars 2026, sous la direction de Shavkat Mirziyoyev et ├á la veille de lÔÇÖA├»d al-Fitr, la c├ęr├ęmonie officielle dÔÇÖinauguration du Centre a eu lieu. Le 13 avril 2026, il a ├ęt├ę inscrit dans le Livre Guinness des records dans la cat├ęgorie du ┬ź plus grand mus├ęe de la civilisation islamique ┬╗.

Historique
Le Centre de civilisation islamique en Ouzb├ękistan a ├ęt├ę cr├ę├ę conform├ęment au d├ęcret pr├ęsidentiel de la R├ępublique dÔÇÖOuzb├ękistan dat├ę du 23 juillet 2017, sign├ę par Shavkat Mirziyoyev. Au cours de plus de huit ann├ęes, ce vaste complexe a ├ęt├ę construit et enti├Ęrement achev├ę. Le 17 mars 2026, ├á la veille de lÔÇÖA├»d al-Fitr, le Centre a ├ęt├ę officiellement inaugur├ę.

fields.title: State Museum of the Temurids
fields.description:
The State Museum of the History of the Timurids is a museum located in the center of Tashkent dedicated to the history of Central Asia during the era of Amir Temur and the dynasty he founded. The museum is included in the ÔÇťList of State Unique Scientific ObjectsÔÇŁ of the Republic of Uzbekistan. It houses more than 5,000 exhibits related to the period of Timur and the Timurid dynasty.

The museum was built on October 18, 1996, in connection with the 660th anniversary of Amir Temur. It is 31 meters high, with a diameter of 70 meters, and contains 500 lighting units.

The museum consists of three floors.

History

The State Museum of the History of the Timurids was established on the initiative of the First President of the Republic of Uzbekistan, Islam Karimov, in 1996 to commemorate the 660th anniversary of Amir TemurÔÇÖs birth. In 2006, on the occasion of the museumÔÇÖs 10th anniversary, the exhibition ÔÇť10 Years of the State Museum of the History of the TimuridsÔÇŁ was opened.

Between 2007 and 2011, a fundamental research project titled ÔÇťThe Study of Written Monuments of the Timurid Period in Uzbekistan and AbroadÔÇŁ was implemented. Within the framework of this project, materials and information related to manuscripts from the era of Amir Temur and the Timurids were collected.

fields.title: Tashkent Television Tower
fields.description: Tashkent Television Tower is the tallest structure in Central Asia and one of the main landmarks of Tashkent. Built in 1985, the tower stands 375 meters high and serves as a broadcasting center for television and radio signals. It also features an observation deck offering panoramic views of the city, making it a popular attraction for visitors and official guests.
fields.description_en: Tashkent Television Tower is the tallest structure in Central Asia and one of the main landmarks of Tashkent. It also features an observation deck offering panoramic views of the city, making it a popular attraction for visitors and official guests.

fields.title: New Uzbekistan Park
fields.description: New Uzbekistan Park is one of TashkentÔÇÖs largest and most modern parks, symbolizing the countryÔÇÖs renewal and future development. The park features vast green areas, fountains, wide walking paths, and a central monument representing national unity and progress. It is a popular destination for residents and visitors, offering a peaceful setting for recreation, cultural events, and official visits.

fields.name: JW Marriott Hotel Tashkent City
fields.subtitle: Conference Venue ÔÇô Grand Ballroom
fields.description:
The Grand Ballroom at JW Marriott Hotel Tashkent offers a sophisticated and spacious setting for high-level conferences, meetings, and official events in the heart of Tashkent City. With approximately 820 m┬▓ of pillar-free space and a ceiling height of around 8.5 meters, the venue provides an elegant and flexible environment suitable for a wide range of event formats. (Marriott)
The ballroom can accommodate up to 600 participants in theatre style, 450 for banquets, and 700 guests for receptions, making it ideal for large-scale international conferences and corporate gatherings. (Marriott)
Equipped with modern audio-visual technology, on-site technical support, and adjacent pre-function areas for registration and networking, the Grand Ballroom ensures a seamless and professional event experience in one of the cityÔÇÖs leading luxury hotels.

fields.title: 2026 WCO Global Canine Forum
fields.title_fr: Forum mondial canin de lÔÇÖOrganisation mondiale des douanes (OMD)
fields.subtitle: Global Canine Forum
fields.body_1: Join the international Canine (K-9) and Customs community in Tashkent, Uzbekistan, from 7ÔÇô9 September 2026 for a global forum dedicated to the development of service dog training and modern border security practices.
fields.body_1_en: Join the international Canine (K-9) and Customs community in Tashkent, Uzbekistan, from 7ÔÇô9 September 2026 for a global forum dedicated to the development of service dog training and modern border security practices.
fields.body_1_fr: Participez au forum mondial r├ęunissant la communaut├ę internationale canine (K-9) et douani├Ęre ├á Tachkent, Ouzb├ękistan, du 7 au 9 septembre 2026, d├ędi├ę au d├ęveloppement de la formation des chiens de service et aux pratiques modernes de s├ęcurit├ę frontali├Ęre.
fields.body_2: The Global Canine Forum (K-9) will bring together Customs administrations, border and law enforcement agencies, experts and partners to exchange experience, demonstrate operational capabilities and discuss the future of canine services in border management. The forum is organized with the participation of the World Customs Organization (WCO) and the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan and will serve as a platform for professional dialogue, practical demonstrations and international cooperation in the field of service dog training.
fields.body_2_en: The Global Canine Forum (K-9) will bring together Customs administrations, border and law enforcement agencies, experts and partners to exchange experience, demonstrate operational capabilities and discuss the future of canine services in border management. The forum is organized with the participation of the World Customs Organization (WCO) and the Customs Committee of the Republic of Uzbekistan and will serve as a platform for professional dialogue, practical demonstrations and international cooperation in the field of service dog training.
fields.body_2_fr:
Le Forum mondial canin (K-9) r├ęunira les administrations douani├Ęres, les services frontaliers et les forces de lÔÇÖordre, ainsi que des experts et partenaires, afin dÔÇÖ├ęchanger des exp├ęriences, de d├ęmontrer des capacit├ęs op├ęrationnelles et de discuter de lÔÇÖavenir des services cynophiles dans la gestion des fronti├Ęres.

Le forum est organis├ę avec la participation de lÔÇÖOrganisation mondiale des douanes (OMD) et du Comit├ę des douanes de la R├ępublique dÔÇÖOuzb├ękistan, et servira de plateforme pour le dialogue professionnel, les d├ęmonstrations pratiques et la coop├ęration internationale dans le domaine de la formation des chiens de service.
fields.body_3: The forum will explore how canine units contribute to detecting illicit goods, combating transnational crime and strengthening border control while facilitating legitimate trade and travel. Each day of the forum will highlight key aspects of modern canine operations.
fields.body_3_en: The forum will explore how canine units contribute to detecting illicit goods, combating transnational crime and strengthening border control while facilitating legitimate trade and travel. Each day of the forum will highlight key aspects of modern canine operations.
fields.body_3_fr: Le forum examinera la mani├Ęre dont les unit├ęs cynophiles contribuent ├á la d├ętection de marchandises illicites, ├á la lutte contre la criminalit├ę transnationale et au renforcement du contr├┤le aux fronti├Ęres, tout en facilitant le commerce et les d├ęplacements l├ęgitimes. Chaque journ├ęe du forum mettra en lumi├Ęre des aspects cl├ęs des op├ęrations cynophiles modernes.
fields.body_4: Sessions will focus on operational practices, training methodologies and the integration of modern technologies in canine services. Through expert discussions, practical demonstrations and knowledge sharing, the forum will highlight how well-trained service dogs and professional handlers remain one of the most effective tools for detecting narcotics, weapons, explosives, wildlife trafficking and other illegal activities.
fields.body_4_en: Sessions will focus on operational practices, training methodologies and the integration of modern technologies in canine services. Through expert discussions, practical demonstrations and knowledge sharing, the forum will highlight how well-trained service dogs and professional handlers remain one of the most effective tools for detecting narcotics, weapons, explosives, wildlife trafficking and other illegal activities.
fields.body_4_fr: Les sessions seront consacr├ęes aux pratiques op├ęrationnelles, aux m├ęthodologies de formation ainsi quÔÇÖ├á lÔÇÖint├ęgration des technologies modernes dans les services cynophiles. ├Ç travers des discussions dÔÇÖexperts, des d├ęmonstrations pratiques et le partage de connaissances, le forum soulignera dans quelle mesure les chiens de service bien dress├ęs et leurs ma├«tres-chiens professionnels demeurent lÔÇÖun des outils les plus efficaces pour la d├ętection des stup├ęfiants, des armes, des explosifs, du trafic dÔÇÖesp├Ęces sauvages et dÔÇÖautres activit├ęs illicites.
